behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the file path (turbopack/crates/turbo-persistence/src/db.rs), the struct (TurboPersistence), the method (is_empty), the existing field (active_write_operation: AtomicBool), and the mutation sites (load_directory, commit). All test-asserted specifics (struct names, method names, file paths, AtomicBool usage, .load()/.store() patterns) are discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact fix: add an AtomicBool field, rewrite is_empty() to use .load(Ordering::Relaxed), and call .store(meta_files.is_empty(), Ordering::Relaxed) at every mutation point. This is a complete implementation specification, not just a problem description.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on very specific implementation details: test_struct_has_atomic_bool checks for a new AtomicBool field in the struct (not active_write_operation), test_isempty_lock_free checks for .load() in the method body, and test_atomic_synced_on_mutation checks for .store() calls containing 'meta_files.is_empty()'. An alternative correct fix using e.g. AtomicU8, a cached field updated differently, or a different sync pattern would fail these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All fail-to-pass tests (test_struct_has_atomic_bool, test_isempty_lock_free, test_atomic_synced_on_mutation, test_relaxed_ordering) are purely text/regex searches on source code. No code is compiled, executed, or behaviorally tested. Every test is essentially 'assert pattern in open(path).read()'.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation (e.g., returning false from is_empty) would fail test_isempty_lock_free which checks for .load() in the method body, and test_struct_has_atomic_bool which requires an AtomicBool field beyond active_write_operation. The anti-stub check in test_isempty_lock_free also rejects hardcoded true/false.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/next.js and solution/solve.sh contains the exact patch. An agent could read solve.sh or the test file itself (tests/test_outputs.py) to discover exactly what patterns are being checked and produce matching code without understanding the bug.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires: (1) new AtomicBool field — tested by test_struct_has_atomic_bool, (2) lock-free is_empty() — tested by test_isempty_lock_free, (3) sync at mutation points — tested by test_atomic_synced_on_mutation. All three explicit requirements are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear separation between pass_to_pass (gates) and fail_to_pass (core behavioral) sections, descriptive function names, and detailed docstrings explaining what each test checks.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task modifies Rust source code and does not produce structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, and commands in the instruction and tests are consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exactly which file to modify, the exact field name pattern (AtomicBool), the exact Ordering (Relaxed), and the exact expression to use (.store(meta_files.is_empty(), Ordering::Relaxed)). This goes far beyond identifying the buggy component.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config edit task.
